package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn

class.name: ContainerLogAppender

method.name: getContainerLogDir
method.doc:
Getter/Setter methods for log4j.
@return containerLogDir.

method.name: setTotalLogFileSize

class.doc: A simple log4j-appender for container's logs.

class.name: ContainerRollingLogAppender

method.name: getContainerLogDir
method.doc:
Getter/Setter methods for log4j.
@return containerLogDir.

class.doc: A simple log4j-appender for container's logs.

class.name: YarnUncaughtExceptionHandler
class.doc:
This class is intended to be installed by calling {@link Thread#setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(UncaughtExceptionHandler)} In the main entry point. It is intended to try and cleanly shut down programs using the YARN Event framework.
Note: Right now it only will shut down the program if a Error is caught, but not any other exception. Anything else is just logged.

method.name: createRMProxy
createRMProxyFederation
getRMDelegationTokenService
checkAllowedProtocols
method.doc:
Verify the passed protocol is supported.
@param protocol protocol.

method.name: getRMAddress
createRMProxy
createRMProxyFederation
createRMProxy
getProxy
createRetryPolicy
createRetryPolicy

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.client.api

class.name: AppAdminClient

method.name: createAppAdminClient
method.doc:
Create a new instance of AppAdminClient.

@param appType application type @param conf configuration @return app admin client

method.name: actionLaunch
method.doc:
Launch a new YARN application.

@param fileName specification of application @param appName name of the application @param lifetime lifetime of the application @param queue queue of the application @return exit code @throws IOException IOException @throws YarnException exception in client or server

method.name: actionStop
method.doc:
Stop a YARN application (attempt to stop gracefully before killing the application). In the case of a long-running service, the service may be restarted later.

@param appName the name of the application @return exit code @throws IOException IOException @throws YarnException exception in client or server

method.name: actionStart
method.doc:
Start a YARN application from a previously saved specification. In the case of a long-running service, the service must have been previously launched/started and then stopped, or previously saved but not started.

@param appName the name of the application @return exit code @throws IOException IOException @throws YarnException exception in client or server

method.name: actionSave
method.doc:
Save the specification for a YARN application / long-running service. The application may be started later.

@param fileName specification of application to save @param appName name of the application @param lifetime lifetime of the application @param queue queue of the application @return exit code @throws IOException IOException @throws YarnException exception in client or server

method.name: actionDestroy
method.doc:
Remove the specification and all application data for a YARN application. The application cannot be running.

@param appName the name of the application @return exit code @throws IOException IOException @throws YarnException exception in client or server

method.name: actionFlex
method.doc:
Change the number of running containers for a component of a YARN application / long-running service.

@param appName the name of the application @param componentCounts map of component name to new component count or amount to change existing component count (e.g. 5, +5, -5) @return exit code @throws IOException IOException @throws YarnException exception in client or server

method.name: enableFastLaunch
method.doc:
Upload AM dependencies to HDFS. This makes future application launches faster since the dependencies do not have to be uploaded on each launch.

@param destinationFolder an optional HDFS folder where dependency tarball will be uploaded @return exit code @throws IOException IOException @throws YarnException exception in client or server

method.name: getStatusString
method.doc:
Get detailed app specific status string for a YARN application.

@param appIdOrName appId or appName @return status string @throws IOException IOException @throws YarnException exception in client or server

method.name: initiateUpgrade
actionUpgradeInstances
actionUpgradeComponents
actionCleanUp
actionUpgradeExpress
actionCancelUpgrade
actionDecommissionInstances

class.doc: Client for managing applications.

class.name: TimelineClient

method.name: createTimelineClient
method.doc:
Creates an instance of the timeline v.1.x client. The current UGI when the user initialize the client will be used to do the put and the delegation token operations. The current user may use {@link UserGroupInformation#doAs} another user to construct and initialize a timeline client if the following operations are supposed to be conducted by that user.
@return the created timeline client instance

method.name: putEntities
method.doc:
Send the information of a number of conceptual entities to the timeline server. It is a blocking API. The method will not return until it gets the response from the timeline server.

@param entities the collection of {@link TimelineEntity} @return the error information if the sent entities are not correctly stored @throws IOException if there are I/O errors @throws YarnException if entities are incomplete/invalid

method.name: putEntities
method.doc:
Send the information of a number of conceptual entities to the timeline server. It is a blocking API. The method will not return until it gets the response from the timeline server. This API is only for timeline service v1.5

@param appAttemptId {@link ApplicationAttemptId} @param groupId {@link TimelineEntityGroupId} @param entities the collection of {@link TimelineEntity} @return the error information if the sent entities are not correctly stored @throws IOException if there are I/O errors @throws YarnException if entities are incomplete/invalid

method.name: putDomain
method.doc:
Send the information of a domain to the timeline server. It is a blocking API. The method will not return until it gets the response from the timeline server.

@param domain an {@link TimelineDomain} object @throws IOException io error occur. @throws YarnException exceptions from yarn servers.

method.name: putDomain
method.doc:
Send the information of a domain to the timeline server. It is a blocking API. The method will not return until it gets the response from the timeline server. This API is only for timeline service v1.5

@param domain an {@link TimelineDomain} object @param appAttemptId {@link ApplicationAttemptId} @throws IOException io error occur. @throws YarnException exceptions from yarn servers.

method.name: getDelegationToken
method.doc:
Get a delegation token so as to be able to talk to the timeline server in a secure way.

@param renewer Address of the renewer who can renew these tokens when needed by securely talking to the timeline server @return a delegation token ({@link Token}) that can be used to talk to the timeline server @throws IOException io error occur. @throws YarnException exceptions from yarn servers.

method.name: renewDelegationToken
method.doc:
Renew a timeline delegation token.

@param timelineDT the delegation token to renew @return the new expiration time @throws IOException io error occur. @throws YarnException exceptions from yarn servers.

method.name: cancelDelegationToken
method.doc:
Cancel a timeline delegation token.

@param timelineDT the delegation token to cancel @throws IOException io error occur. @throws YarnException exceptions from yarn servers.

class.doc: A client library that can be used to post some information in terms of a number of conceptual entities. This client library needs to be used along with Timeline V.1.x server versions. Refer {@link TimelineV2Client} for ATS V2 interface.

class.name: TimelineReaderClient

method.name: createTimelineReaderClient
method.doc:
Create a new instance of Timeline Reader Client.
@return instance of Timeline Reader Client.

method.name: getApplicationEntity
getApplicationAttemptEntity
getApplicationAttemptEntities
getContainerEntity
getContainerEntities

class.doc: A client library that can be used to get Timeline Entities associated with application, application attempt or containers. This client library needs to be used along with time line v.2 server version.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.event

class.name: AbstractEvent
class.doc: Parent class of all the events. All events extend this class.

class.name: AsyncDispatcher

constructor.name: AsyncDispatcher
constructor.doc: Set a name for this dispatcher thread. @param dispatcherName name of the dispatcher thread

class.doc: Dispatches {@link Event}s in a separate thread. Currently only single thread does that. Potentially there could be multiple channels for each event type class and a thread pool can be used to dispatch the events.

interface.name: Dispatcher
interface.doc: Event Dispatcher interface. It dispatches events to registered event handlers based on event types.

interface.name: EventHandler
interface.doc:
Interface for handling events of type T
@param <T> parameterized event of type T

next

method.name: initialize
initInternal
method.doc: Derived classes initialize themselves using this method. @param conf the Configuration

method.doc: Get the remote root log directory. @return the remote root log directory path

method.name: getRemoteRootLogDirSuffix
method.doc: Get the log aggregation directory suffix. @return the log aggregation directory suffix

method.name: getFileControllerName
method.doc: Get the name of the file controller. @return name of the file controller.

closeWriter

postWrite

getNodeFilesOfApplicationDirectory
renderAggregatedLogsBlock
getApplicationOwner
getApplicationAcls
verifyAndCreateRemoteLogDir
method.doc: Verify and create the remote log directory.

method.name: createAppDir

getRemoteAppLogDir
getOlderRemoteAppLogDir
aggregatedLogSuffix

field.name: TLDIR_PERMISSIONS
field.doc: Permissions for the top level directory under which app directories will be created.

class.doc: Base class to implement Log Aggregation File Controller.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.security

class.name: AMRMTokenIdentifier
class.doc: AMRMTokenIdentifier is the TokenIdentifier to be used by ApplicationMasters to authenticate to the ResourceManager.

class.name: AccessRequest
class.doc: This request object contains all the context information to determine whether a user has permission to access the target entity. user : the user who's currently accessing accessType : the access type against the entity. entity : the target object user is accessing. appId : the associated app Id for current access. This could be null if no app is associated. appName : the associated app name for current access. This could be null if no app is associated. remoteAddress : The caller's remote ip address. forwardedAddresses : In case this is an http request, this contains the originating IP address of a client connecting to a web server through an HTTP proxy or load balancer. This parameter is null, if it's a RPC request.

class.name: ContainerTokenIdentifier

constructor.name: ContainerTokenIdentifier
constructor.type: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ContainerId, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Resource, long, int, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Priority, long

constructor.name: ContainerTokenIdentifier
constructor.type: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ContainerId, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Resource, long, int, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Priority, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.LogAggregationContext
constructor.deprecated: Use one of the other constructors instead.

constructor.name: ContainerTokenIdentifier
constructor.type: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ContainerId, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Resource, long, int, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Priority, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.LogAggregationContext, java.lang.String

constructor.name: ContainerTokenIdentifier
constructor.type: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ContainerId, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Resource, long, int, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Priority, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.LogAggregationContext, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.api.ContainerType

constructor.name: ContainerTokenIdentifier
constructor.type: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ContainerId, int, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Resource, long, int, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Priority, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.LogAggregationContext, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.api.ContainerType, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ExecutionType

constructor.name: ContainerTokenIdentifier
constructor.type: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ContainerId, int, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Resource, long, int, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Priority, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.LogAggregationContext, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.api.ContainerType, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ExecutionType, long

constructor.name: ContainerTokenIdentifier
constructor.type: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ContainerId, int, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Resource, long, int, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.Priority, long, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.LogAggregationContext, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.api.ContainerType, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ExecutionType, long, java.util.Set<java.lang.String>

constructor.name: ContainerTokenIdentifier
constructor.doc: Default constructor needed by RPC layer/SecretManager.

method.name: getRMIdentifier
method.doc: Get the RMIdentifier of RM in which containers are allocated. @return RMIdentifier

method.name: getContainerType
method.doc: Get the ContainerType of container to allocate. @return ContainerType

method.name: getExecutionType
method.doc: Get the ExecutionType of container to allocate @return ExecutionType

method.name: getVersion
method.doc: Get the Container version @return container version

method.name: getNodeLabelExpression
method.doc: Get the node-label-expression in the original ResourceRequest. @return node label expression.

class.doc: TokenIdentifier for a container. Encodes {@link ContainerId}, {@link Resource} needed by the container and the target NMs host-address.

constructor.name: NMTokenIdentifier
constructor.doc: Default constructor needed by RPC/Secret manager

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.security.client

class.name: BaseClientToAMTokenSecretManager
class.doc: A base {@link SecretManager} for AMs to extend and validate Client-RM tokens issued to clients by the RM using the underlying master-key shared by RM to the AMs on their launch. All the methods are called by either Hadoop RPC or YARN, so this class is strictly for the purpose of inherit/extend and register with Hadoop RPC.

class.name: ClientToAMTokenSecretManager
class.doc: A simple {@link SecretManager} for AMs to validate Client-RM tokens issued to clients by the RM using the underlying master-key shared by RM to the AMs on their launch. All the methods are called by either Hadoop RPC or YARN, so this class is strictly for the purpose of inherit/extend and register with Hadoop RPC.

class.name: RMDelegationTokenIdentifier

constructor.name: RMDelegationTokenIdentifier

class.doc: Delegation Token Identifier that identifies the delegation tokens from the Resource Manager.

constructor.name: TimelineDelegationTokenIdentifier

method.name: computeChecksum
getChecksum

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.state

class.name: InvalidStateTransitionException
class.doc: The exception that happens when you call invalid state transition.

class.name: InvalidStateTransitonException
class.doc: @deprecated Use {@link InvalidStateTransitionException} instead.

interface.name: MultipleArcTransition

method.name: transition

interface.doc: Hook for Transition. Post state is decided by Transition hook. Post state must be one of the valid post states registered in StateMachine.

interface.name: SingleArcTransition

method.name: transition

interface.doc: Hook for Transition. This lead to state machine to move to the post state as registered in the state machine.

class.name: StateMachineFactory

constructor.name: StateMachineFactory

method.name: addTransition
addTransition
addTransition
addTransition
addTransition
installTopology
method.doc:
@return a StateMachineFactory just like {@code this}, except that if you won't need any synchronization to build a state machine
Note that the returned StateMachineFactory is a distinct object.
This method is part of the API.
The only way you could distinguish the returned StateMachineFactory from {@code this} would be by measuring the performance of the derived {@code StateMachine} you can get from it.
Calling this is optional. It doesn't change the semantics of the factory, if you call it then when you use the factory there is no synchronization.

method.name: make
generateStateGraph

interface.name: StateTransitionListener

method.name: preTransition
postTransition

interface.doc: A State Transition Listener. It exposes a pre and post transition hook called before and after the transition.

See the License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.util

class.name: AbstractLivelinessMonitor
class.doc: A simple liveliness monitor with which clients can register, trust the component to monitor liveliness, get a call-back on expiry and then finally unregister.

class.name: ApplicationClassLoader
class.doc: This type has been deprecated in favor of {@link org.apache.hadoop.util.ApplicationClassLoader}. All new uses of ApplicationClassLoader should use that type instead.

class.name: BoundedAppender

method.name: append
length
toString

interface.name: Clock
interface.doc: A simple clock interface that gives you time.

class.name: ConverterUtils

method.name: getPathFromYarnURL
convertFromYarn
convertFromYarn

class.doc: This class contains a set of utilities which help converting data structures from/to 'serializableFormat' to/from hadoop/nativejava data structures.

class.name: MonotonicClock

method.name: getTime
method.doc: Get current time from some arbitrary time base in the past, counting in milliseconds, and not affected by settimeofday or similar system clock changes. @return a monotonic clock that counts in milliseconds.

class.doc: A monotonic clock from some arbitrary time base in the past, counting in milliseconds, and not affected by settimeofday or similar system clock changes. This is appropriate to use when computing how much longer to wait for an interval to expire. This function can return a negative value and it must be handled correctly by callers. See the documentation of System#nanoTime for caveats.

class.name: ResourceCalculatorProcessTree

constructor.name: ResourceCalculatorProcessTree

method.name: initialize
method.doc: Initialize the object. @throws YarnException Throws an exception on error.

method.name: updateProcessTree
method.doc:
Update the process-tree with latest state.
Each call to this function should increment the age of the running processes that already exist in the process tree. Age is used other API's of the interface.

method.name: getProcessTreeDump
getVirtualMemorySize
method.doc:
Get the virtual memory used by all the processes in the process-tree.
@return virtual memory used by the process-tree in bytes, {@link #UNAVAILABLE} if it cannot be calculated.

method.name: getRssMemorySize
method.doc:
Get the resident set size (rss) memory used by all the processes in the process-tree.
@return rss memory used by the process-tree in bytes, {@link #UNAVAILABLE} if it cannot be calculated.

method.name: getVirtualMemorySize
method.doc:
Get the virtual memory used by all the processes in the process-tree that are older than the passed in age.
@param olderThanAge processes above this age are included in the memory addition @return virtual memory used by the process-tree in bytes for processes older than the specified age, {@link #UNAVAILABLE} if it cannot be calculated.

method.name: getRssMemorySize
method.doc:
Get the resident set size (rss) memory used by all the processes in the process-tree that are older than the passed in age.
@param olderThanAge processes above this age are included in the memory addition @return rss memory used by the process-tree in bytes for processes older than specified age, {@link #UNAVAILABLE} if it cannot be calculated.

method.name: getCumulativeCpuTime
method.doc:
Get the CPU time in millisecond used by all the processes in the process-tree since the process-tree was created
@return cumulative CPU time in millisecond since the process-tree created, {@link #UNAVAILABLE} if it cannot be calculated.

method.name: getCpuUsagePercent
method.doc:
Get the CPU usage by all the processes in the process-tree based on average between samples as a ratio of overall CPU cycles similar to top. Thus, if 2 out of 4 cores are used this should return 200.0. Note: UNAVAILABLE will be returned in case when CPU usage is not available. It is NOT advised to return any other error code.
@return percentage CPU usage since the process-tree was created, {@link #UNAVAILABLE} if CPU usage cannot be calculated or not available.

method.name: checkPidPgrpidForMatch
method.doc: Verify that the tree process id is same as its process group id. @return true if the process id matches else return false.

method.name: getResourceCalculatorProcessTree

class.doc: Interface class to obtain process resource usage NOTE: This class should not be used by external users, but only by external developers to extend and include their own process-tree implementation, especially for platforms other than Linux and Windows.

class.name: SystemClock
class.doc:
Implementation of {@link Clock} that gives the current time from the system clock in milliseconds.
NOTE: Do not use this to calculate a duration of expire or interval to sleep, because it will be broken by settimeofday. Please use {@link MonotonicClock} instead.

class.name: UTCClock
class.doc: Implementation of {@link Clock} that gives the current UTC time in milliseconds.

timelineServiceV1_5Enabled
generateFlowNameTag
shortenFlowName
generateFlowVersionTag
generateFlowRunIdTag

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.webapp.hamlet2

class.name: ButtonType
class.doc: Values for button types

class.name: Dir
class.doc: Values for the %18n dir attribute (case-insensitive)

class.name: EOpt
class.doc: Element options. (whether it needs end tag, is inline etc.)

class.name: InputType
class.doc: %InputType (case-insensitive)

class.name: LinkType
class.doc: %LinkTypes (case-insensitive)

class.name: Media
class.doc: %MediaDesc (case-sensitive)

class.name: Method
class.doc: Values for form methods (case-insensitive)

class.name: Scope
class.doc: %Scope (case-insensitive)

class.name: Shape
class.doc: %Shape (case-insensitive)

package.doc: This package is the replacement for org.apache.hadoop.yarn.webapp.hamlet. The old package is using _ as a one-character identifier, which is banned from JDK9.
